text.title: product-development-trends Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-development-trends as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Over-indexing on US/SF Bay Area sources without requiring EU or APAC primary sources.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs are trained on US-centric data. Force at least one EU and one APAC primary source per trend or the brief is regionally biased.

failure-mode.description: Using raw WebSearch results ranked by SEO rather than by publication date.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SEO-optimised blog spam ranks above primary research. Always sort by published date in your scraper before summarizing.

failure-mode.description: "Rapid pivots, quarterly cycles" assertions without named organizations or concrete shipping evidence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is a popular LLM completion regardless of evidence. Treat any unsourced cadence claim as hallucinated.

failure-mode.description: Cross-functional team claims usually cite the same 2–3 secondary sources (HBR, McKinsey blog) as independent evidence.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Do not let three citations to the same article inflate confidence.

failure-mode.description: Missing human-in-the-loop checkpoint before writing into .aidocs/product_docs/.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A human must accept the final scored signal table. Without this gate, hallucinated tool/vendor names land in deliverables.
